reasoning: The effective yield for the Euro High Yield Index closed August at a local high of 5.08%, up significantly from 4.85% in mid-August. This late-August run-up is correlated with a shift in ECB rate expectations, as markets priced out September rate cuts in favor of holds through December based on resilient economic data. However, at 5.08%, the index is sitting at a near-term local peak. Short-term statistical forecasts consistently suggest mean reversion (FRED trend model predicts 4.98 by Sep 7). Because we are measuring against this elevated water mark, the probability of exceeding it in the very short term (Sep 7, Sep 30) is below 50% (around 40-44%). For the longer-term horizons (Nov 2025, Feb 2026), uncertainty fundamentally increases; yields could adjust upward if the ECB pivots hawkish, or downward if the rate cutting cycle resumes. Therefore, probabilities revert smoothly toward the uninformative prior of 50%.
